circular.title: Master Circular – Basel III Capital Regulations
circular.summary: This Master Circular provides a consolidated framework for Basel III capital adequacy standards for Scheduled Commercial Banks in India. It defines the minimum capital requirements, internal process for assessing capital adequacy, and mandatory public disclosures. Banks must maintain a Minimum Total Capital of 9% and a Common Equity Tier 1 ratio of 5.5%, supplemented by a 2.5% Capital Conservation Buffer.

clauses.text: The Basel III capital regulations were implemented in India with effect from April 1, 2013 and have been fully implemented as on October 1, 2021.
A bank shall comply with the capital adequacy ratio requirements at two levels: (a) consolidated (“Group”) level and (b) standalone (“Solo”) level.
Banks shall maintain a minimum Pillar 1 Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 9% on an on-going basis
Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital must be at least 5.5% of risk-weighted assets (RWAs).
Goodwill and all other intangible assets should be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 capital.
clauses.summary: Deduction of goodwill and intangible assets

clauses.text: Claims on the central government will attract a zero risk weight.
clauses.summary: Risk weight for central government claims

clauses.text: Claims that meet all four qualifying criteria (Orientation, Product, Granularity, and Low value) shall be assigned a risk weight of 75%.
Lending to individuals for residential property shall be risk weighted based on LTV ratios
clauses.summary: Risk weight for residential property lending

clauses.text: The unsecured portion of NPA shall be risk-weighted at 150% when specific provisions are less than 20% of the outstanding amount.
Claims on Venture Capital Funds will attract a risk weight of 150%.
clauses.summary: Risk weight for Venture Capital Funds

clauses.text: The total risk-weighted off-balance sheet credit exposure is calculated by converting notional amounts to credit equivalents using Credit Conversion Factors (CCFs).
clauses.summary: Calculation of off-balance sheet credit exposure

clauses.text: Banks should use the chosen credit rating agencies and their ratings consistently for each type of claim.
No transaction in which CRM techniques are used should receive a higher capital requirement than an otherwise identical transaction where such techniques are not used.
All documentation used in collateralised transactions and guarantees must be binding on all parties and legally enforceable in all relevant jurisdictions.
Banks are required to manage the market risks in their books on an ongoing basis and ensure that the capital requirements are maintained at the close of each business day.
Banks in India shall compute the capital requirements for operational risk under the Basic Indicator Approach (BIA).
Banks shall hold capital for operational risk equal to the average over the previous three years of 15% of positive annual gross income.
Banks were advised to develop and put in place, with the approval of their Boards, an ICAAP commensurate with their size, level of complexity, and risk profile.
Every bank shall have an ICAAP. It shall be prepared on a solo basis at every tier and at the level of the consolidated bank.
The ICAAP Document should reach the RBI latest by end of the first quarter (i.e., April-June) of the relevant financial year.
Banks should have a formal disclosure policy approved by the Board of Directors that addresses the bank’s approach for determining what disclosures it will make.
Banks are required to make Pillar 3 disclosures at least on a half-yearly basis (Quarterly for Tables DF-2, DF-3, and DF-4).
Banks are required to maintain a CCB of 2.5%, comprised of CET1 capital, above the regulatory minimum CRAR of 9%.
The minimum Leverage Ratio shall be 4% for Domestic Systemically Important Banks (D-SIBs) and 3.5% for other banks.
Banks are required to publicly disclose their Basel III leverage ratio on a consolidated basis.
The CCCB may be maintained in the form of CET1 capital only, and the amount may vary from 0 to 2.5% of total RWAs.
CCCB shall be maintained on a solo basis as well as on consolidated basis.

obligations.text: Maintain a minimum Pillar 1 Capital to Risk-weighted Assets Ratio (CRAR) of 9% on an ongoing basis
obligations.plain_english: Banks must maintain a minimum CRAR of 9%

obligations.text: Maintain a minimum Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital of 5.5% of risk-weighted assets (RWAs)
obligations.plain_english: Banks must maintain a minimum CET1 of 5.5%

obligations.text: Deduct Goodwill and all other intangible assets from Common Equity Tier 1 capital
obligations.plain_english: Banks must deduct goodwill and intangible assets from CET1

obligations.text: Maintain a Capital Conservation Buffer (CCB) of 2.5% of RWAs in the form of CET1 capital
obligations.plain_english: Banks must maintain a CCB of 2.5%

obligations.text: Establish a Board-approved policy on Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process (ICAAP)
obligations.plain_english: Banks must establish an ICAAP policy

obligations.text: Submit the ICAAP Document to the RBI in the prescribed format
obligations.plain_english: Banks must submit their ICAAP document to RBI

obligations.text: Maintain a minimum Leverage Ratio of 4% for D-SIBs and 3.5% for other banks
obligations.plain_english: Banks must maintain a minimum leverage ratio

obligations.text: Disclose Pillar 3 information at least half-yearly (with key tables disclosed quarterly)

prohibitions.text: No transaction in which CRM techniques are used should receive a higher capital requirement than an otherwise identical transaction where such techniques are not used
prohibitions.plain_english: Banks cannot use CRM techniques to increase capital requirements

definitions.definition: The highest quality component of capital, primarily common shares and retained earnings, ensuring bank solvency
definitions.plain_english: CET1 is the highest quality capital that ensures a bank's solvency

definitions.definition: Capital instruments like Perpetual Non-Cumulative Preference Shares (PNCPS) that absorb losses on a going-concern basis
definitions.plain_english: AT1 is a type of capital that absorbs losses on a going-concern basis

definitions.definition: Subordinated debt and general provisions that absorb losses in a 'gone-concern' (liquidation) situation
definitions.plain_english: Tier 2 capital absorbs losses in a liquidation situation

definitions.definition: The risk that a party to a contractual agreement will be unable to meet their obligations
definitions.plain_english: Credit risk is the risk that a party will not meet their obligations

definitions.definition: The risk of losses arising from movements in market prices (interest rates, equities, foreign exchange)
definitions.plain_english: Market risk is the risk of losses due to market price movements

definitions.definition: The risk of loss resulting from inadequate or failed internal processes, people, and systems or from external events
definitions.plain_english: Operational risk is the risk of loss due to internal or external factors

definitions.definition: Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process; a bank's own assessment of its risks and required capital
definitions.plain_english: ICAAP is a bank's assessment of its risks and capital requirements

definitions.definition: Supervisory Review and Evaluation Process; the RBI's evaluation of a bank's ICAAP
definitions.plain_english: SREP is the RBI's evaluation of a bank's ICAAP

definitions.definition: Loan-to-Value ratio; the loan amount as a percentage of the value of the mortgaged property
definitions.plain_english: LTV ratio is the loan amount as a percentage of the property value

definitions.definition: Assets weighted according to their risk level to determine minimum capital requirements
definitions.plain_english: RWA is assets weighted by risk level to determine capital requirements

references.circular_number: Master Direction – Classification, Valuation and Operation of Investment Portfolio (2023)
Master Direction – Reserve Bank of India (Securitisation of Standard Assets) Directions, 2021
